examples: Roman scholar Cicero and the Greek poet Pindar both reference the Wheel of Fortune.
It's actually how Cicero remembered his speeches, how medieval scholars memorized entire texts.
Cicero said of Socrates that, "he called philosophy down from the skies and into the lives of men."
Writers deprecated the Latin of the medieval philosophers and tried to write like Cicero and Virgil.
Cicero did not use ad hoc as we do, nor the economist's jargon ex post.
